page: The Vultr voc-g-2c-8gb-50s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 shared vCPUs (2 physical cores with 1 thread per core), 8.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core, and a bundled 50 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks show a mixed profile. The instance achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, though its multi-core performance falls into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests indicate average performance for large-block read and write operations, but weak performance for small-block cached reads. Passmark evaluations place the CPU score, extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests in the bottom 25%. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also rank in the lower tiers, with web serving throughput falling into the bottom 10%.

Given the shared CPU allocation and the balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance represents a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated compute resources. It is suitable for basic general-purpose tasks, lightweight single-threaded applications, small-scale development environments, and low-traffic web serving where high throughput is not critical.
description: The Vultr voc-g-2c-8gb-50s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring an AMD EPYC Milan processor with 2 shared vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks, though its multi-core and overall Passmark CPU scores reside in the lower tiers. Memory performance is average for large blocks but weak for small cached reads. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput are also positioned in the lower percentiles. This resource profile offers a cost-effective entry point for general-purpose workloads, development environments, and lightweight single-threaded applications that do not demand dedicated CPU resources or high-throughput database performance.
